title: Solution Architect
description:
overview:

rei systems’ mission is to deliver reliable and innovative technology solutions that empower federal, state, local, and nonprofit organizations to achieve their missions. for over 35 years, we’ve helped our customers solve complex challenges, make data\-driven decisions, streamline processes, and improve citizen services. as a 100% employee\-owned company, we are all directly invested in delivering excellence. with a fierce commitment to mission impact, we ensure measurable outcomes that align with our customers’ strategic goals.  

rei is also leading the industry in the integration of artificial intelligence across federal capture, delivery, and customer engagement. we leverage ai\-driven research, opportunity intelligence, competitive assessments, and customer insight tools to elevate our win strategies and accelerate decision\-making. this role will work in an environment where ai is embedded into the capture lifecycle—and will be expected to use ai tools to enhance capture quality, speed, and precision.  

the ideal candidate is equally comfortable engaging executives, leading product demos, understanding customer workflows, and translating business needs into compelling solution narratives.  

we offer the same commitment to our employees by providing professional development, meaningful projects, and flexibility to spend time with family and friends. we believe employees are at their best when fulfilled in both their professional careers and their personal lives. learn more at www.reisystems.com.  

employees voted rei systems a washington post top workplace in 2015, 2016, 2018, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025!
responsibilities:
**position overview**
rei systems is seeking a highly experienced and delivery\-focused lead solution architect to provide end\-to\-end architectural leadership for large\-scale enterprise modernization initiatives supporting mission\-critical business, regulatory, and operational systems.
the successful candidate will serve as the principal technical authority across application architecture, cloud transformation, ai enablement, security, devsecops, and solution delivery. this role requires a strategic thinker who can define future\-state architectures, drive modernization roadmaps, influence stakeholders, and lead cross\-functional teams in delivering secure, scalable, and innovative solutions.
this is a hands\-on leadership role requiring deep technical expertise, strong consulting and communication skills, and a proven ability to deliver enterprise solutions from concept through production deployment.**key responsibilities****architecture leadership*** own end\-to\-end solution architecture for enterprise applications and modernization programs.
* define target\-state architectures, technology strategies, and transformation roadmaps.
* establish architecture standards, governance processes, design patterns, and technical guardrails.
* ensure alignment across business, application, data, integration, security, infrastructure, and operational domains.
* lead architecture reviews, design workshops, and technical decision\-making forums.

**cloud modernization \& digital transformation*** lead migration and modernization efforts from legacy and on\-premises platforms to aws cloud\-native architectures.
* define cloud adoption strategies including microservices, apis, event\-driven architectures, containerization, and serverless solutions.
* develop hybrid architecture approaches supporting coexistence of cloud and on\-premises environments.
* drive application modernization, technical debt reduction, and platform transformation initiatives.

**ai \& innovation leadership*** identify and implement opportunities to leverage generative ai, large language models (llms), intelligent automation, and emerging technologies.
* define enterprise ai adoption strategies, governance frameworks, and responsible ai practices.
* guide integration of ai\-enabled capabilities that improve productivity, user experience, operational efficiency, and decision\-making.
* stay current with emerging technologies and recommend innovation opportunities aligned with business objectives.

**delivery leadership*** partner with program managers, product owners, engineering leads, and executive stakeholders to ensure successful project delivery.
* drive accountability across development, testing, devsecops, security, and operations teams.
* identify technical risks and develop mitigation strategies.
* ensure architecture decisions support schedule, budget, performance, security, and operational objectives.
* provide technical leadership throughout design, development, testing, deployment, and production support.

**stakeholder engagement*** serve as a trusted advisor to business and technical stakeholders.
* communicate complex technical concepts to executive leadership and customer organizations.
* lead architecture briefings, solution workshops, and executive presentations.
* influence strategic decisions through data\-driven recommendations and sound technical judgment.
* support solutioning, proposal development, and technical strategy initiatives as needed.

**team leadership \& mentorship*** mentor architects, engineers, and technical leads.
* promote engineering excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.
* foster a culture of ownership, accountability, collaboration, and customer success.
* support development of high\-performing technical teams.


qualifications:
**experience*** 15\+ years of experience designing and delivering enterprise\-scale software solutions.
* 10\+ years of architecture experience in complex enterprise environments.
* demonstrated hands\-on technical expertise with the ability to actively contribute where needed. the individual should be capable of independently analyzing the existing codebase, identifying architectural and design challenges, proposing effective solutions, and, when necessary, directly contributing to development efforts to ensure project deliverables are met on time and with quality.
* demonstrated success leading cloud transformation and modernization initiatives.
* proven experience delivering mission\-critical systems with high availability, security, scalability, and performance requirements.
* experience leading geographically distributed teams and cross\-functional organizations.
* strong client\-facing consulting and stakeholder management experience.

**technical expertise****application \& integration architecture**
experience designing enterprise solutions using:* java/spring ecosystem
* restful apis and api\-first architectures
* microservices and event\-driven architectures
* modern web application frameworks
* enterprise integration patterns and distributed systems

**cloud \& platform architecture**
strong expertise in:* aws cloud\-native services and architecture patterns
* containerization and orchestration technologies
* serverless architectures
* infrastructure as code (terraform or equivalent)
* hybrid cloud and modernization strategies

**data architecture**
experience with:* relational and nosql database technologies
* data migration and modernization initiatives
* enterprise data integration architectures
* data performance optimization and scalability
* data governance and security principles

**security architecture**
experience implementing:* identity and access management (iam)
* oauth2, openid connect, and single sign\-on (sso)
* secure api and application architectures
* zero trust principles
* security and compliance frameworks

**devsecops \& engineering excellence**
experience establishing:* ci/cd pipelines and deployment automation
* infrastructure as code practices
* automated quality and security controls
* cloud\-native devsecops practices
* software delivery and operational excellence frameworks

**ai \& emerging technologies**
experience evaluating and implementing:* generative ai and large language models (llms)
* ai\-assisted business workflows and automation
* ai governance and responsible ai practices
* enterprise ai integration patterns
* emerging technology adoption strategies

**preferred technology stack**
experience with the following technologies is highly desirable:* java 17, spring boot 3, angular
* aws ecs, fargate, lambda, api gateway, rds, dynamodb
* oracle database
* terraform, docker
* jenkins, git, sonarqube, nexus
* pingfederate, oracle access manager (oam), oracle internet directory (oid)
* aws cloudwatch, cloudtrail, x\-ray

**leadership competencies****executive presence*** strong executive communication and presentation skills.
* ability to influence stakeholders across business and technical organizations.
* excellent written and verbal communication skills.

**strategic thinking*** ability to align architecture decisions with business goals and mission outcomes.
* strong analytical, problem\-solving, and decision\-making skills.
* ability to balance long\-term strategy with near\-term delivery objectives.

**ownership \& accountability*** takes ownership of outcomes and delivery success.
* drives decisions and resolves ambiguity.
* builds accountability while fostering collaboration.

**innovation \& design thinking*** forward\-thinking approach to technology and architecture.
* passion for modernization, innovation, and continuous improvement.
* ability to evaluate emerging technologies and identify business value.

**team leadership*** proven ability to mentor and develop technical talent.
* experience building collaborative, high\-performing teams.
* ability to influence without direct authority.

**preferred qualifications*** aws solutions architect – professional certification
* togaf certification
* safe architect or safe program consultant (spc)
* experience supporting federal government or highly regulated environments
* experience with fedramp, fisma, nist, and zero trust initiatives
* experience implementing ai/ml, generative ai, rag, or agentic ai solutions
* experience supporting healthcare, regulatory, or compliance\-focused systems
* experience with enterprise content management, document management, or reporting platforms

 **education:** bachelor’s degree in computer science, data analytics, business intelligence, economics, statistics, or mathematics**clearance:** ability to obtain and maintain a clearance is preferred.**location:** hybrid (2 days per week or as needed in the rei sterling office).**eeo statement:** rei systems is an equal opportunity employer. in our workplace, we seek, we welcome, and respect diversity in all of its forms.

title: Engineering AI Product Engineer (LLM Systems & DevOps)
description:
ai product engineer (llm, react \& cloud engineering)position overview

**location:** remote (u.s. based only \- prefer pacific northwest)

**employment type:** full\-time contractor

**experience level:** mid\-level engineer (3–6 years)

**work authorization**

candidates must be authorized to work in the united states without current or future employer sponsorship. incluud is not able to sponsor or transfer employment visas, including h\-1b, tn, e\-3, opt, cpt, or other employment\-based work authorization programs at this time.

**role:** 

incluud is seeking an ai product engineer to help design, build, and deploy the next generation of ai\-powered saas products. this role combines software engineering, ai development, cloud infrastructure, and devops practices to create scalable, secure, and reliable solutions that deliver real business value.

you will work closely with product, engineering, and leadership teams to build ai\-driven capabilities using large language models (llms), modern cloud technologies, and responsive web applications. this is an ideal role for someone who enjoys working across the entire technology stack—from user interface development to backend apis, ai integrations, and cloud infrastructure.

key responsibilitiesai product development

* design and build ai\-powered features using large language models (claude, claude design, gpt, or similar technologies).
* develop workflows that leverage ai for document analysis, compliance, loyalty programs, recommendations, data summarization, and business automation.
* implement prompt engineering, context management, and output validation techniques.
* evaluate ai outputs for quality, accuracy, and reliability while minimizing hallucinations and incorrect responses.
* create guardrails, validation layers, and feedback mechanisms to improve ai performance.

front\-end development (react)

* design and develop modern, responsive web applications using react.
* build intuitive user interfaces that provide exceptional user experiences.
* integrate front\-end applications with backend apis and ai services.
* collaborate with product teams to rapidly prototype and deliver new features.
* optimize application performance, accessibility, and responsiveness across devices.

software engineering

* develop and maintain backend services, apis, and microservices.
* build and optimize restful apis that connect ai services, databases, and enterprise applications.
* write clean, maintainable, and well\-documented code.
* troubleshoot and resolve issues across the full technology stack, including ui, apis, ai services, and infrastructure.
* participate in code reviews and contribute to engineering best practices.

devops \& cloud infrastructure

* deploy and manage applications in aws, azure, or google cloud environments.
* build and maintain ci/cd pipelines for automated testing and deployments.
* utilize docker and kubernetes for containerized application deployment and orchestration.
* automate infrastructure provisioning and configuration management using tools such as terraform and ansible.
* monitor system performance, reliability, and availability using modern monitoring and logging platforms.

security \& identity

* support secure multi\-tenant saas architectures.
* implement and maintain identity and access management solutions using oauth, sso, auth0, azure ad, or similar technologies.
* ensure compliance\-conscious design aligned with soc 2, gdpr, and security best practices.
* participate in incident response, troubleshooting, and disaster recovery planning.

required qualificationstechnical experience

* 3–6 years of professional software engineering experience.
* strong experience with react and modern javascript/typescript development.
* proficiency in at least one backend language such as python, c\#, java, go, or similar.
* experience building and deploying production software applications.
* hands\-on experience integrating ai/llm services into real\-world applications.

ai \& llm experience

* experience using claude, gpt, or similar ai platforms.
* understanding of prompt engineering and ai workflow design.
* experience integrating llm apis into software products.
* familiarity with ai output validation and quality assurance practices.

cloud \& devops

title: Senior AWS Cloud Architect
description:
**job description: senior aws cloud architect**





**location: wichita, ks (hybrid)**





**position overview:**





we are seeking an experienced and visionary **cloud architect** to spearhead our enterprise cloud transformation. in this role, you will design and execute our migration strategy from legacy on\-premises infrastructure to amazon web services (aws). beyond migration, you will establish robust cloud governance frameworks, define operational guardrails for our infrastructure teams, and architect scalable data solutions that fuel our business intelligence, analytics, and artificial intelligence (ai) initiatives.





**key responsibilities:**





**. cloud migration \& modernization:** lead the architectural assessment, strategy, and execution of transitioning legacy, on\-premises workloads to aws. utilize frameworks such as the aws well\-architected framework to ensure secure, high\-performing, resilient, and efficient infrastructure.


**. cloud networking:** partner with network architect to design and implement cloud networking architecture. set up vpc peering. evaluate the need for, and implement, a direct connect with aws.


**. cloud governance \& guardrails:** design, implement, and maintain enterprise\-wide cloud landing zone and governance policies. define identity and access management (iam) strategies, security baselines, and resource lifecycle policies to guide the systems engineering and operations teams in secure, standardized resource provisioning.


**. infrastructure as code (iac):** champion automation by establishing iac standards and ci/cd pipelines for infrastructure deployment, ensuring the systems engineering team can safely and consistently manage cloud resources.


**. data \& ai architecture:** architect scalable, secure data platforms—including data lakes and modern data warehouses—to aggregate diverse data streams. ensure the underlying infrastructure optimized for high\-performance data analytics, reporting pipelines, and advanced ai/ml modeling.


**. cost optimization \& performance tuning:** implement finops practices to monitor, analyze, and optimize cloud spend while ensuring optimal system performance and scalability.





**required qualifications:**





**. experience:** 7\+ years in it infrastructure/architecture, with at least 3\+ years specifically focused on aws cloud architecture and large\-scale migrations.


**. aws expertise:** deep technical knowledge of core aws services (ec2, s3, rds, vpc, iam, route 53\) and specialized data/analytics services (aws glue, redshift, athena, emr, sagemaker, amazon bedrock).


**. automation \& provisioning:** proficiency with infrastructure as code tools (e.g., terraform, aws cloudformation, aws cdk) and configuration management.


**. governance frameworks:** proven experience setting up aws organizations, aws control tower, and service control policies (scps) for enterprise compliance.


**. certifications:** one or more of the following: aws certified solutions architect – professional or associate, aws devops engineer – professional or associate





**preferred skills:**



. familiarity with containerization and orchestration (docker, amazon ecs/eks).



. strong understanding of data governance, data privacy regulations, and security compliance frameworks.



. excellent collaboration skills to bridge the gap between technical operations teams and data science/analytics business units.

join our team as the expert you are now and create your future.


we are looking for a hands\-on and adaptable ai/ml engineer to join our ai practice. you’ll help design, build, and deploy intelligent systems that leverage a range of technologies—from machine learning to generative ai and agent\-based architectures—to solve complex, real\-world problems.  

this role spans the full ai solution lifecycle: data preparation, model development, application integration, and ongoing evaluation. you will collaborate closely with architects, and domain experts to ensure the ai solutions you help build are intelligent, reliable, and aligned with client goals.  

we work across industries and use cases, from early\-stage prototypes to production\-ready deployments, and we're continuously exploring new ai paradigms—including ai agents, agent orchestration layer, model chaining, and self\-evaluating systems. if you’re excited by emerging ai technologies, fast\-moving teams, and applied problem\-solving, this is the role for you.**key responsibilities**

* ability to work on cross\-functional teams, balance multiple projects, and communicate effectively with technical and non\-technical audiences

title: Full Stack Software Engineer - AI Applications
description:
job id
66003
category
enterprise technology
location
long beach, california
work type
on\-site

**who you are**
---------------

**an agent orchestrator, not a typist.** you don't want to be a faster keyboard. you want to be a manager of agents — handing off the heavy lifting, reviewing the output, and keeping your hands on the architecture. the ide is a cockpit for orchestration, not a text editor.

**productively lazy.** your dream workflow: describe the requirement, let the agent build it, verify, ship, next. you automate anything a human shouldn't be doing twice. your biggest bottleneck should be deciding *what* to build — while ai executes the *how*.

**fundamentals first.** data structures, algorithms, distributed systems, networking — you understand the machine, not just the library that wraps it. when a framework breaks, you fix it. when ai gives you the wrong answer, you catch it. orchestrating agents only works if you can tell good output from garbage.

**first\-principles thinker with vision.** you break problems to their core, question the assumptions, and rebuild. a software engineer's job is to architect solutions, not wrestle with syntax. you don't copy an architecture because "that's how it's done" — you ask why and decide if there's a better way.

**high agency.** you don't wait for perfect specs or permission. you find a path, propose it, and move. large organizations have walls; you figure out which ones to go through, around, or remove — and you do it constructively.

**bias for action.** requirements will be messy and priorities will shift. you ship v1, learn, and iterate instead of living in design review.

**what you'll do**
------------------

* **orchestrate agents to build.** use ai as your default way of working — agents do the heavy lifting, you direct, review, and harden. you set the bar for how the team builds with ai.
* **build the ai use cases.** design and ship ai\-powered applications and agents — rag pipelines, llm integrations, agentic workflows. understand what's happening under the hood and make it work in production, at scale.
* **ship cloud\-native systems on gcp.** design, deploy, operate. you own the architecture, the infrastructure\-as\-code, and the ci/cd pipeline. no throwing code over the wall.
* **connect systems that don't want to be connected.** build the integration layer across enterprise saas platforms. expect messy apis, legacy constraints, and creative problem\-solving.
* **automate what shouldn't be manual.** if a human repeats it, you write the code — or point an agent at it — to stop it.
* **make other engineers faster.** build the tools, agent workflows, and guardrails that remove friction. developer productivity is a multiplier.

**what you bring**
------------------

* **python** — deep. you write production systems, not just scripts.
* **javascript / typescript** — react, node.js, or equivalent. a real frontend and a real api. front\-end strength is a big plus.
* **ai engineering** — llms, embeddings, vector stores, rag. comfortable building agents and evaluation pipelines. bonus for fine\-tuning or eval work.
* **ai\-assisted development** — fluent with modern ai coding tools and agent workflows, and clear\-eyed about where they help and where they don't.
* **gcp** — cloud run, cloud functions, gke, bigquery, cloud storage. deployed and operated, not just tutorials.
* **databases** — relational and nosql. you know when to use what, and why.
* **apis \& microservices** — designed and built restful services at scale.
* **git, iac \& ci/cd** — terraform, cloud build, or equivalent. reproducible, version\-controlled infrastructure and disciplined code review.

**requirements**
----------------

* bs in computer science, electrical engineering, or a related field. ms is a plus, not a substitute for shipping software.
* 2\+ years building and deploying full\-stack applications in production.
* 2\+ years on cloud\-native platforms (gcp preferred).
* demonstrable experience building ai agents or applications — show us what you've built, not what you've read.

**why this role**
-----------------


ford is a 120\-year\-old company moving fast on ai, and that's the real challenge: building world\-class ai applications inside a large enterprise with real constraints — security, compliance, legacy systems. the engineers who thrive here treat those constraints as problems to solve, not reasons to stop. if you want a place where everything is already figured out, this isn't it. if you're a 10x thinker who wants to build something that matters — and have the grit to push through the hard parts — let's talk.

* 


you may not check every box, or your experience may look a little different from what we've outlined, but if you think you can bring value to ford motor company, we encourage you to apply!


as an established global company, we offer the benefit of choice. you can choose what your ford future will look like: will your story span the globe, or keep you close to home? will your career be a deep dive into what you love, or a series of new teams and new skills? will you be a leader, a changemaker, a technical expert, a culture builder…or all of the above? no matter what you choose, we offer a work life that works for you, including:

* immediate medical, dental, vision and prescription drug coverage
* flexible family care days, paid parental leave, new parent ramp\-up programs, subsidized back\-up child care and more
* family building benefits including adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, fertility treatments, and more
* vehicle discount program for employees and family members and management leases
* tuition assistance
* established and active employee resource groups
* paid time off for individual and team community service
* a generous schedule of paid holidays, including the week between christmas and new year's day
* paid time off and the option to purchase additional vacation time


for a detailed look at our benefits, click here: https://fordcareers.co/gsr


this position ranges from salary grade 6\-8 and ranges from $85,400\-$232,700\.


final determination of salary grade will be based on candidate's skills and experience, and base salary will be set within the applicable range according to job scope, responsibility and competitive market value.


visa sponsorship is available for this position.


relocation assistance is provided for this position.


candidates for positions with ford motor company must be legally authorized to work in the united states. verification of employment eligibility will be required at the time of hire.


we are an equal opportunity employer. all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status or protected veteran status. in the united states, if you need a reasonable accommodation for the online application process due to a disability, please call 1\-888\-336\-0660\.

title: Software Developer
description:
dayforce is a global human capital management (hcm) company headquartered in toronto, ontario, and minneapolis, minnesota, with operations across north america, europe, middle east, africa (emea), and the asia pacific japan (apj) region.

our brand promise \- makes work life better™ \- reflects our commitment to employees, customers, partners and communities globally.
  
  

about the opportunity
  
the dayforce product development team is seeking an experienced software developer to help build the next generation of human capital management (hcm) solutions. as a member of the workforce management team, you will contribute to a market\-leading solution that helps organizations improve efficiency through time and attendance tracking and intelligent employee scheduling. you will participate in the full software development lifecycle, including design, analysis, prototyping, development, testing, deployment, and support. working closely with product owners, designers, implementation teams, and fellow engineers, you will help deliver innovative functionality while contributing to a collaborative and high\-performing development culture.
  
what you’ll get to do

* participate actively in agile development processes and team collaboration activities.


skills and experience we value
  
* strong understanding of object\-oriented design principles (solid).
* 2\+ years of experience developing software using c\#/.net, including async/await and linq.
* experience with modern .net technologies (e.g., .net 6, 7, or 8\).
* experience building production user interfaces using react and typescript.
* working knowledge of modern web technologies including html5, css3, svg, json, rest apis, and web apis.
* strong sql server skills including indexing, query optimization, and transaction management.
* experience with docker containers and ci/cd pipelines such as github actions or azure devops.
* experience deploying applications to cloud platforms such as azure, aws, or gcp.
* experience developing and maintaining automated testing frameworks, including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing.
* experience using ai\-powered development tools such as github copilot, codeium, or sourcegraph cody.
* strong analytical, problem\-solving, and troubleshooting abilities.
* excellent communication and collaboration skills.
* ability to work independently and as part of an agile development team.


what would make you stand out
  
* experience applying design patterns and performance optimization techniques.
* experience with asp.net core/mvc and api design.
* understanding of multithreading, concurrency, and thread synchronization.
* experience with event\-driven architectures, messaging platforms (kafka, azure service bus), and caching technologies such as redis.
* experience with observability tools including opentelemetry, application insights, or prometheus.

what’s in it for you
  
dayforce is fueled by the diversity of our talented employees. we are an equal opportunity employer and consider and embrace all individuals and what makes them unique. we believe our employees should be happy and healthy, with peace of mind and a sense of fulfillment.
  
we encourage individuals to apply based on their passions.
  
dayforce employees and their families are eligible to participate in the following benefits programs: medical, dental, vison, and life insurance. dayforce employees are also eligible to participate in a 401k plan (plus match) and a global employee stock purchase plan. employees also receive unlimited time away from work (in lieu of accrued vacation time), 10 paid us holidays, up to 80 hours of paid sick time and 17 weeks of paid parental leave, subject to the terms of the applicable policy or program.
  
with a commitment to community impact, including volunteer days and our charity, dayforce cares we provide opportunities for you to thrive both in your career and personal life. our focus is not just on your job but on supporting you to be the best version of yourself.
  
about the salary ranges
  
  

please note that the salary range mentioned in this job description should serve simply as a guide. the final compensation offered may vary based on a variety of factors, including bonuses and/or incentives, or a candidate’s experience, skills, budget and location. our company is committed to providing a fair, equitable, and competitive package that reflects the value an individual brings to the organization.

description:
overview:
**summary**
